entry.title: Re: eDocument: Italy E-Invoicing - Process Flow
entry.content:
Hi Randy, actually i found only the official H2G from SAP from here: https://api.sap.com/package/eDocumentElectronicInvoicingforItaly/documents 

Unfortunately even with these ones we were not fully able to complete the setup. Did you manage in the meantime to implement the package from SAP? Is there some tip on what to look out for, especially regarding the custom domain (cert authentication required for the whole maschine, change of the domain name for all iFlows, etc.)? Is this really necessary?

Kind regards,

Martin

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content: Hi, I am Mathew from Austria, originally from India. Recently, I changed my career to SAP consulting and am starting with the MM module. Happy to join the community.

entry.title: Packaging material items created although TVTY-KZPGN initial
entry.content:
Dear community,

I am facing the following issue: We are generating packaging material items from handling units in outbound deliveries. For some packaging materials, we don't want to generate items. Hence, we are using packaging material types where we leave the field TVTY-KZPGN blank. As far as I understand the documentation for this field, no items should be generated if it is blank. However, the system still creates those items.

Any experience with this? Any solution proposals?

Thanks and best regards, Michael

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 1
entry.content:
Here is my try to create a "Production order created" event.

{
  "type": "sap.s4.beh.productionorder.v1.ProductionOrder.Created.v1",
  "datacontenttype": "application/json",
  "specversion": "1.0",
  "source": "m32z",
  "subject": "Production Order 1000206 created",
  "id": "QgEK7wzuHtqdhJwqCS+VOA==",
  "time": "2024-02-08T12:00:00Z",
  "data": {
    "ProductionOrder": "1000206",
    "ProductionOrderType": "PP01",
    "ProductionPlant": "HD00"
  }
}

entry.title: Re: February Developer Challenge - CAP Plugins: Week 4
entry.content: Late submission but this week I have been busy playing with Flexible Programming Model 🙂

entry.title: Re: Adjust column width according to the data through CDS view
entry.content:
Hi Ian,

It seems there's no such cssDefaults:

I guess you were referring to CAP CDS which seems to have it with different syntax:

I couldn't find it in the ABAP CDS Documentation too (under lineItem or elsewhere): UI Annotations 

Conclusion is that it's not available via ABAP CDS UI annotation. Any expert can confirm this?

Those looking for a solution can have a look here for some other options suggested outside CDS: Setting the Default Column Width

entry.title: Re: CO88 and OKG8 Relationship?
entry.content:
Hello Olga, For 1 , I mean to say In OKG8 POCI/POCS must be tagged in combination with some RA cost element category 31, Check to which line id that cost element mapped in OKG4, now in OKG5 check cost element mapped to this line id (now is there any cost elelemt that is used in your production order or not).

It may also possible settlement check all items in OKG8 (Check if RA cost element maintained against POCI/POCS or not).

Regards,

Sumesh Gupta

entry.title: Re: Change Material in Sales Order Line Item Using BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE
entry.content:
• Are you allowed to change this field with VA02 (what is line item status, are there followng documents, etc.)
  • In recent versions there is a MATERIAL_LONG field for long material number
  • Search and read also OSS notes such as 3260628 - BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE does not set MATNR_CHANGED

entry.title: Re: SAP Java Buildpack 2: Jakarta-Based, Fresh and New!
entry.content:
Hi @andreas_paul ,

Memory Calculator V1 is not supported by SJB 2 because V1 uses SAP JVM (which is based on Java 8).  SJB 2 does not carry SAP JVM but only SapMachine JRE 17.

Note: Even in SJB 1, Memory Calculator V1 is not the default one, and Memory Calculator v2 is the recommended one. See: Memory Calculator

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Recruiting: New Career Site Job Search and Job Layout
entry.content:
Hi all,

just a quick follow-up on radius search. SAP confirmed to us, that the standard feature "radius search" is currently not available when using Unified Data Model. Their proposed workaround is to check a possibility to have a custom code.

In the next release, they will introduce a new Google job map, so maybe this might include a new radius search functionality for unified data model.

entry.title: SAP Forms Service by Adobe with WebUI S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello,

We are implementing the Grantor Management solution from SAP on the S/4 system. This solution foresees in several user interfaces for the end-user:

  • Web User Interface (based on FIORI 3.0)
  • Fiori apps

The Fiori apps for Grantor are not official native Fiori apps but are more WCF kind of apps (WEBCUIF).

During the process of handling the Grantor transactions is creating output (correspondence) that is send to the customer. So for this, the user should be able to generate a document (brief/letter) that is retrieving data from the S/4 back end and pre-fill the document.

Once the document is created by the system, the user should be able to edit this document before sending it to the customer.For this process of creating and editing documents we would like to propose SAP Forms by Adobe since we were told this is the future-proof solution provided by SAP for this purpose.

The Grantor solution is not new, it was already released years ago on SAP CRM system. This SAP CRM system user interface was called the CRM WebClient.
In the past we had several discussions with SAP regarding the possibility to use SAP Forms by Adobe with the SAP CRM WebClient. It was then determined that this would not work due to old techniques in SAP CRM and it would only work with S/4HANA.

Now since Grantor is now running on S/4HANA we would like to know if it is now possible to use SAP Forms by Adobe with the Web User interface (based on FIORI 3.0) and/or the Fiori apps (WCF type of Fiori apps)?

Why we would like to position SAP Forms by Adobe in combination with Grantor is because of the capabilities of the SAP Forms solution:

  • Create interactive forms that look exactly like their paper counterparts.
  • Annotate PDF documents and collaborate on PDF document reviews.
  • Capture data using forms and import the data directly into the SAP system.
  • Allow users to digitally sign PDF documents.
  • Embed other file formats inside PDF documents as attachments.

So the question we have is, is it possible to use SAP Forms by Adobe in combination with the Web User Interface and/or Fiori apps for Grantor on S/4HANA?

Best regards,

Pieter Schepers

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Recruiting: New Career Site Job Search and Job Layout
entry.content:
@maseitner we are analysing to start the implementation and what we are still in doubt is how to update the open JR ( around nearly 300) with the new standard fields.

@LisaRaff I saw your comments about regarding this. Have you succeeded in doing a mass update via integration center?

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Recruiting: New Career Site Job Search and Job Layout
entry.content:
Hi @HangTRAN ,

no, unfortunately we did not succeed so far with an upload via integration centre. SAP support could not help us with this issue, either. If you find a solution for this we would be eager to hear about it!

Kind regards,

Lisa

entry.title: Re: PO/INVOICE PRICE DEVIATIONS
entry.content: You can set this in vendor master in accounting view. But you have to set the limit in this configuration.

entry.title: Re: Composite Widgets SAC - Responsive Story
entry.content:
Hi Daniel,

You will be able to add composite into story responsive page in QRC2/2024 Release.

 

thanks and best regards

Jie

entry.title: How to trigger sending orders to SCPI
entry.content:
Hello,

I am working on SAP commerce cloud 2211 and composable storefront 6.x. I am creating orders from the frontend, they are saved in the commerce backend. I want my orders to be sent to SCPI, so I created all the outbound configurations e.g. consumed destinations, http distinations, channel, stream etc. But the sending is not triggered.

In the code I see the action SapCpiOmsOrderOutboundAction but it is not part of any business process. I mean when I search I can not find it in any XML definition.

Is the XML definition included in another extension?

I also read, i should install ysapcpiomsfulfillment, which I did. But it includes just strategy classes with code similar to the SapCpiOmsOrderOutboundAction and it is also not triggered.

So, the questions now are

1- How to trigger the sending?

2-What is the difference between SapCpiOmsOrderOutboundAction and the classes in ysapcpiomsfulfillment? why do they have similar code and when should each of them be used?

Here are the extensions, that I use

Hello,

I am working on SAP commerce cloud 2211 and composable storefront 6.x. I am creating orders from the frontend, they are saved in the commerce backend. I want my orders to be sent to SCPI, so I created all the outbound configurations e.g. consumed destinations, http distinations, channel, stream etc. But the sending is not triggered.

In the code I see the action SapCpiOmsOrderOutboundAction but it is not part of any business process. I mean when I search I can not find it in any XML definition.

Is the XML definition included in another extension?

I also read, i should install ysapcpiomsfulfillment, which I did. But it includes just strategy classes with code similar to the SapCpiOmsOrderOutboundAction and it is also not triggered.

So, the questions now are

1- How to trigger the sending?

2-What is the difference between SapCpiOmsOrderOutboundAction and the classes in ysapcpiomsfulfillment? why do they have similar code and when should each of them be used?

Here are the extensions, that I use

<extension name="magnithordermanagement"/> based on yacceleratorordermanagement
<extension name="magnithsapcpiomsfulfillment"/> based on ysapcpiomsfulfillment
<extension name="sapcpicustomerexchangeb2b"/>
<extension name="sapcpiorderexchangeb2b"/>

<extension name="ordermanagementwebservices"/>
<extension name="b2bordermanagementwebservices"/>
<extension name="sapcpiorderexchange"/>
<extension name="sapcpiadapter"/>

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: EWM pick pass pack and pass with pick by cart app pos...
entry.content:
Hi,

ok, this would need to be complete checked, by setting it up in order to see what limitations you might run into. And all I describe would be the standard, without modifications.

But in general you would use a pick, pack, & pass scenario for this. Also the "normal products" would come from an activity area which is part of the PPP scenario (otherwise the pick HU would not move to the second area for the next picking).

If you then use the pick by cart app for one of the picking parts, is a different question. The pick by cart app is a fancy version of the RF dialog, technically you use the RF framework. But of course the pick by cart app is meant to pick into several pick HUs at the same time. Which is possible in a PPP scenario as well, but usually gets messy. Why do you want to use the pick by cart app?

OK, probably because of this: " In this area, however, it is not multi-order picking but picking per box (or picking slip). " So in the first area you have a warehouse order which includes WTs for different deliveries, separated by different pick HUs, in the second area you now want to split the next WTs into different WOs.

Yeah, that is something which needs to be tested. The main controlling part for the pick by cart app is the resource type. And you need a WOCR which makes the combination of WTs, but keeps them in different pick HUs. That is just the WOCR. But if this works in a PPP scenario, you need to try (at least if no-one here says that they have done it already). The system must be able to create a higher level WO (which happens in a PPP scenario), but the lower level WOs do not all belong together.

Brgds

Juergen

---
All the above is no official SAP statement.

Want to learn EWM?
Check for EWM courses: https://training.sap.com/trainingpath/Applications-Extended+Warehouse+Management-EWM+in+S4HANA
Get a SAP Learning Hub Subscription: https://training.sap.com/learninghub

And it is EWM. NOT eWM - Duh!
And if your question includes the word "transfer order" - do NOT tag the question with Extended Warehouse Management!

entry.title: Way of functioning of SAP Jco Library
entry.content:
Dear SAP community,

We have implemented a JCo Server, which uses tRFC and the JCoFunction IDOC_INBOUND_ASYNCHRONOUS. The JCoServerFunctionHandler as well as the JCoServerTIDHandler have custom implementations for saving IDocs with CRUD operations into the database.

During internal tests we noticed, that for instance, if we would throw a RuntimeException (e.g. the database connection has been lost) in the commit() method of the JCoServerTIDHandler the transaction will not be reprocessed.

The same behavior could be observed, if we just throw a RuntimeException in the handleRequest(JCoServerContext jcoServerContext, JCoFunction jcoFunction) of the JCoServerFunctionHandler implementation. 

In both cases we receive an error in the log file, but SAP does not seem to send the IDoc again or check with checkTID(JCoServerContext serverContext, String transactionID) if the IDoc has been processed already.

So my lack of understanding results in three questions:

  • Is there no feedback with regards to the information in case the IDoc has been received?
  • Do you need to call a specific JCoFunction to signal SAP the IDoc has not been successfully received and needs to be sent again in a later try?
  • Is this in general not something I can do with the JCo Java Library and it needs to be configured on the SAP system to handle feedback sent back (e.g. through a RuntimeException) to the SAP system (I do not know, if there is a response from the SAP library in this case)

I would appreciate any valuable input with regards to this question as I am currently a bit stuck to cover all necessary edge cases to ensure data consistency.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Is direct SAP HANA database connectivity permitted in RISE - SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Our objective is to integrate SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition (RISE) with our Data Warehouse system, utilizing ODBC-based connectivity to establish a direct connection with the SAP HANA database.

We are keen to ascertain the feasibility of establishing direct ODBC connectivity to the SAP HANA database within the SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud RISE Edition. Your insights on this matter would be greatly valued as we proceed with our integration plans.

Kind regards, Vijay

entry.title: Re: How To Upload Data to a Public or Private Planning Version Using a File Upload Custom Widget Wi
entry.content:
Hi @ginot 

Unfortunately this is not possible.

Best regards

Gerd

entry.title: Re: User without global accounts associated in SAP BTP Cockpit - trial

entry.title: What’s New for SAP Build Apps – Mobile Authentication
entry.content:
Greetings Builders! This week we have released a significant feature for our customers: mobile authentication.

We have now enabled SAP Mobile Services, which authenticates end users in your native mobile applications and provides access to integrations and services from SAP Business Technology Platform. 

SAP Build Apps uses SAP Cloud Identity Services – Identity Authentication (IAS) for user authentication in the cloud. For more information on this topic, see the article: What is Identity Authentication?

Now with the ability to harness the power of Identity Authentication, you can increase the enterprise readiness of your company’s mobile apps by ensuring seamless integration with SAP Business Technology Platform. This feature allows you to reuse destinations of backend systems, which are also used for web applications and many other BTP services.

To get started with taking advantage of this feature, verify that you have Subaccount entitlements of at least 1 unit for Mobile Services (standard plan) as a prerequisite. 

After configuring mobile authentication in your BTP Cockpit and your build settings, you can build your first mobile app with authentication services enabled. Your users will be prompted with a first-time setup, after which they'll need to provide a single access code each time they log in.

👉For instructions on getting started with mobile authentication, see the documentation: SAP Build Apps – SAP Mobile Services.

We look forward to seeing our customers unlock more possibilities by enabling mobile authentication in their apps! Feel free to share any feedback or questions about this release in the comments.

Check out what else is on the horizon for SAP Build Apps this year in the Roadmap Explorer.

To see all recent SAP Build Apps releases, visit the What’s New Documentation.

entry.title: A day in the life of a Customer Success Partner – helping customers on their cloud journey
entry.content:
Before we start: Our main protagonist Torsten

Torsten is a 35-year-old engaged SAP Customer Success Partner (CSP) for a major automotive supplier. He is a dedicated and tech-savvy professional who acts as an intermediary between SAP and the customer. His main goal is to ensure the smooth implementation of the customer’s cloud solutions and their optimal operation. In doing so, he uses a variety of SAP tools and services and regularly agrees with the customer. His activities run under the SAP Preferred Success cloud service. I thought sharing Thorsten’s story is a good illustration how SAP Cloud Services look like in real life.

Figure: Illustration of different activities in SAP Preferred Success

Preparation with Onboarding and Cloud Mindset Workshop

The day starts with the activities around onboarding the customer. Torsten ensures that the customer has access to all relevant resources. The customer gets everything he & she need to be successful independently. In addition, after onboarding, the customer knows how to contact Torsten and how to tackle open topics together.

As part of the onboarding, the cornerstone for the collaboration between the Customer Success Partner and the customer is fixed. The first regular dates and synch-meetings are set.

The Cloud Mindset Workshop is the entry point to the world of SAP Preferred Success services. Together, the cultural and organizational change that forms the basis for the successful implementation and operation of SAP cloud solutions is reflected here. Mindset stands for values, beliefs, norms which influence our behaviors. Working with Cloud Software due to the different nature of cloud compared to on-premise software needs a change in mindset. For further reading see this whitepaper from SAPs Change Management team.

Learning Recommendations to develop SAP Knowledge

With SAP Preferred Success, the customer receives five licenses from the SAP Learning Hub, the comprehensive learning platform for SAP products and technologies. Torsten ensures that the customer uses these licenses optimally. He recommends appropriate learning content, learning paths, live-sessions, training systems and/or certifications. For SAP Preferred Success there is even an own learning portal with communities by Edition and learning content around the SAP Preferred Success Services.  

Support the implementation of SAP cloud solutions

In the implementation phase of a SAP Cloud solution, Torsten offers a number of services, including solution launch checks related to integration, extensibility, identity & access management, embedded analytics, UX exploration, or SAP Cloud ALM. He can select them from a service catalog as required by his customer. Torsten wants to achieve a smooth implementation process and an optimal go-live in cooperation with the customer.

The regular cycle of adoption, operation & optimization

Software features must be reliable. At the same time, existing and new features must adapt to the changing needs of an organization. For this purpose, new features and options are constantly being rolled out over regular releases. 

Depending on the customer’s needs, Torsten also introduces additional tools and resources, such as the Release Adoption and Scope Dependency Tool (RASD). Or he uses other services such as guidance for regression tests or support for a post-upgrade test.

Individual SAP Preferred Success Services tailored to each phase and situation

Torsten provides specific services and checks based on the customer’s phase and requirement. These include Q-Gate Review Sessions, various checks such as Continuous Quality Checks (CQCs) or Solution Launch Checks.

In addition, there can always be customer-specific ad hoc advisory sessions where Torsten can access an entire catalog. Depending on the cloud solution, these services may also vary.

360 degree care with SAP Preferred Success

Torsten plays a very similar role to a concierge service or a personal trainer. His activities span the entire solution life cycle at the customer and include diverse tasks such as coordination between different teams at the customer, at SAP, and at SAP partners. 

He ensures that critical support messages are processed and, if necessary, escalated. He also takes care of the necessary documents and forms to request and deliver SAP Preferred Success services.

As part of SAP Preferred Success, customers benefit from enhanced service level agreements (enhanced SLA), i.e. support messages are processed with priority. This applies to the entire SAP Preferred Success contract period and therefore to each phase of the cloud solution.

In the regular review meeting, he discusses questions such as:

  • Where can I find what resources?
  • How can I activate specific scope items?

If Torsten cannot answer a question directly, he contacts a suitable SAP expert. If necessary, he also organizes appropriate meetings to enable the discussion regarding questions from his customer.

Sometimes it helps to show his customer how to best use SAP’s existing services and resources. For example how to leverage the SAP Preferred Success Community, SAP Learning Hub, Whats New Viewer, or the SAP Help Portal.

Strategic retrospectives and future plans in gobal business reviews

Strategic topics are discussed in the Business Review Meetings once or twice a year. Participants are usually Senior & executive management of the customer and the respective business process owners.

Summary and customer feedback

“Thanks to Torsten and SAP Preferred Success, our company has been able to take full advantage of the SAP Cloud solution” – is just one of the quotes from a satisfied customer.

This concludes another successful working day for Torsten, who ensures that his customers achieve the desired results every day.  

I hope this blog provided you some insights into the world of managing SAP Cloud Software and SAP Preferred Success.

For more information, visit the SAP Preferred Success website. Looking forward for your questions or comments below this blog.

entry.title: How to remove out-of-use Managed System? - SAP Solution Manager 7.2
entry.content:
We have some out of use managed system need remove from SAP Solution Manager. What is the steps that I need to follow?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to remove out-of-use Managed System? - SAP Soluti...
entry.content:
Please follow the step below to decommission a system:

  1. Go to SOLMAN_SETUP
  2. Select Managed System Configuration 
  3. Select a technical system
  4. Click on Advanced Operations
  5. Decommissioning

 

These steps are described on KBA 0001952567 - How to remove no longer relevant managed systems from Solution Manager

entry.title: Re: create inline a filter table with new abap syntax
entry.content: Hi Sandra! I'm not limited to standard tables. The intention of my question was to find a short way to create a filter like described - without creating extra tables with secondary keys and doing some mapping. The mentioned tables articles and productions will only contain 1-5 entries, so I shouldn't get a performance issue.

entry.title: Re: Recap — SAP Data Unleashed 2024
entry.content: 👍

entry.title: SAP Solution Roadmap for Oil, Gas & Energy Industry
entry.content:
My Dear Customers, Partners, Colleagues in the below blog you will get an outlook on the SAP Oil, Gas & Energy Solution Roadmap for coming next 2-3 years.

This blog will be covering the following roadmaps items for the Oil, Gas & Energy Industry :

  • Commodity Trading and Risk Management
  • Upstream Business
  • Downstream Business

Commodity Trading & Risk Management (CTRM) for Oil, Gas & Energy Industry

You can find the detailed information on Commodity Trading & Risk Management for Oil, Gas & Energy on SAP Road Map Explorer. The highlighted items in above screenshot are the Oil, Gas & Energy specific roadmap items.

Upstream Business for Oil, Gas & Energy: Future roadmap for Upstream business for the Oil, Gas & Energy Industry highlights the innovations & improvement for SAP products Joint venture account (JVA), Production Revenue accounting (PRA) & Field logistics. Below is the combined roadmap for SAP Solutions supporting Upstream business for the Oil, Gas & Energy Industry.

You can find the detailed information on Upstream Solutions for the Oil, Gas & Energy Industry on SAP Road Map Explorer.

Downstream Business for Oil, Gas & Energy: Future roadmap for Downstream business for the Oil, Gas & Energy Industry highlights the innovations & improvement for SAP products. Some of the highlights are the block chain use cases, Transportation Management (TM) & Traders Scheduler’s Workbench (TSW) integration for Shipment costing.

You can find the detailed information on Downstream Solutions for the Oil, Gas & Energy Industry on SAP Road Map Explorer.

Possible future developments listed in roadmap items are subject to change and may be changed by SAP at any time for any reason without notice. This document is provided without a warranty of any kind, either express or implied, including but not limited to, the implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement.

Thanks to my colleagues Richard Philcox and Robert Davis II for their contribution to this blog.

If you have any questions, comments etc. please do not hesitate to contact us.

entry.title: Re: what is full name of SAP MRP LSF
entry.content: Losfertigung is a German word which means Batch Production.

entry.title: Re: Performance analysis in the SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello Ayman, 

thanks for sharing the insight information. 

Could you tell how we can identify 'schema name' for the particular sql script generated from Data/business builder objects?

You kept the schema name “Einkauf_Demo” before the table name? From where is it come ?

Thanks for the help in advance.

entry.title: A Waring message is displayed when creating an error message in RAP
entry.content:
Hi experts! 

I had to create a save_modified method in my Service and there creating an error message.

APPEND VALUE #( %tky = VALUE #( %is_draft  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on        
                                HeaderUuid = lv_header_uuid )           
                %msg = new_message( id         = lx_runtime->id             
                                    number     = lx_runtime->number         
                                    severity   = lx_runtime->severity_error )
) TO reported-header.

The UI displays a warning message with the option to cancel or save the dataset.

I would expect an error message and no option to save the dataset.

Does anybody knows how to achieve this?

Thanks and regards, Sebastian

entry.title: Re: What's New for SAP Build Apps - Enhanced Fusion Development
entry.content: @EsmeeX nice! Authorisation possible as well (Groups/Roles from IdP)?

entry.title: How to extend Fiori App F1053A in a SAP Cloud private edition version 2022
entry.content:
Hi.

We need to add some fields to the App manage vendor master F1053A in the Area showed in the screenshot below. 

We have tried via the Adapt UI, but this is not possible in the area for Company Codes.

 

We need to know what to be done to achieve this. 

Best regards Anna Marie

entry.title: Re: Blog it Forward – Pierre Col
entry.content:
Hi Pierre, I have loved learning about your story, since 2020 I have been interviewing the people I have met in the SAP ecosystem, with a special focus on the SME segment, and this blog feels as real as each of my "Teas con... .".

Do you speak Spanish, even a little? I am glad to know that two of your three children are from my neighboring country, Colombia, I send greetings to all of you and wish you many blessings.

Thank you very much for sharing your story with us, I admire and respect you.

Best regards,

Erick

entry.title: Labs Talk Sustainability Series Part 1/3
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce the commencement of our Labs Talk Sustainability Series, marking the beginning of a three-part exploration into the intricacies of sustainable practices. Hosted by Sven Haiges and Andreas Neumeier, the inaugural episode features special guests Carina Legl and Miguel Valenzuela from SAP.

In this live stream, scheduled for March 14th at 14:00 CET, we will delve into critical topics shaping the sustainable landscape. The discussion will traverse Aspects of Sustainability, Circularity, SAP Recommerce, Key Industries engaging in circularity, and the transformative Impact of GenAi on Circularity. Moreover, the session will shed light on the strategic alliances that play a pivotal role in driving sustainability initiatives forward.

Mark your calendars for March 14th and join us on this intellectual journey towards a greener and more sustainable future.

entry.title: Re: Announcement: New release of content and central entry point for SAP BTP Solution Diagrams
entry.content:
Glad to see an update to these! For me the main question remains whether we can expect Visio support for this? It remains a key tool for a lot of our diagrams and having a published stencil would be great. I'd settle for just being able to fetch a zip of decent PNGs though. 🙂

Should we assume that we won't see a return to icons for SAP SaaS solutions? We seem to only have "branded texts" these days. The old icons for SFSF, Concur, Ariba and the like were useful to maintain a consistent style.

entry.title: Re: A day in the life of a Customer Success Partner – helping customers on their cloud journey
entry.content: @ThomasJenewein Thanks for this.  I'm fortunate to work with several very good and easy to work with CSPs.  This role is huge between SAP and the customer.

entry.title: Re: E-Mail attached documents linked in QM02 (Quality Notifications)
entry.content:
For (very) recent versions, a few tools may now be available (compatibility GOS / ArchiveLink)

Else in addition to GOS tools, look also for ArchiveLink tools and FM such as those of group OPTB for example.

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to create variants for the job that re...
entry.content:
Hello,
no in the standard SAP System there is no such an option, since you should always know what you release into other branches. Additionally it would cause inconsistency if there are conflicts between the branches.

Best regards
Pedro
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Recruiting: New Career Site Job Search and Job Layout
entry.content: @HangTRAN we did not go live with the funtionality yet, but at the moment our recruiters would have to set the new job location manually.

entry.title: Re: Back to basics with SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) - part 7
entry.content:
Here are the resources mentioned in this episode:

  • International Women's Day https://www.google.com/search?q=International+Women%27s+Day 
  • SAP Developer News: International Day of Women and Girls in Science Roundtable Discussion https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V06Ck94GYQM 
  • The GitHub repo for this series, now updated https://github.com/qmacro/capb2b 
  • This week's SAP Developer News episode https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6Mx-1cZmumI
  • March Developer Challenge (on CloudEvents) Week 1 https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/march-developer-challenge-cloudevents-week-1/td-p/13629129 
  • SAP CodeTalk is back in 2024 https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/sap-codetalk-is-back-in-2024/ba-p/13596847 
  • (Capire) Common Types and Aspects - Type Country https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/cds/common#type-country 
  • Origin of the phrase "into the drink" https://english.stackexchange.com/questions/119621/origin-of-in-the-drink 
  • The SAP CDS Language Support extension for VS Code https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=SAPSE.vscode-cds 
  • (Capire) Common Reuse Aspects https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/cds/common#common-reuse-aspects
  • (Capire) The namespace Directive https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/cds/cdl#namespace
  • (Capire) The context Directive https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/cds/cdl#context 
  • (Capire) Aspect CodeList https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/cds/common#aspect-codelist 
  • (Capire) Adding Initial Data in .csv Files https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/get-started/in-a-nutshell#adding-initial-data-in-csv-files 
  • ISO 3166-1 Country Codes standard https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1 
  • CDS Common Content NPM package https://www.npmjs.com/package/@sap/cds-common-content 
  • (Capire) Use cds add data https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/databases#use-cds-add-data 
  • Autovivification https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autovivification 
  • The Difference between the UK, Great Britain & England Explained https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rNu8XDBSn10 
  • A Tree Sitter plugin for CDS, incl. support for Neovim https://github.com/cap-js-community/tree-sitter-cds 
  • Language Server Protocol https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Language_Server_Protocol 
  • (Capire) Declaring Localized Data https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/localized-data#declaring-localized-data 
  • (Capire) Domain Modeling - Associations https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/domain-modeling#associations 
  • Physicist Niels Bohr's famous quote about predictions https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33383160/

entry.title: Re: Send Payment Summary file by email from F110
entry.content: If I were you, I'd start by tracing / debugging the F110 transaction to identify the call to Function Module JOB_CLOSE FM to add a recipient, or look for implicit enhancement in the Abap report ?

entry.title: Re: Growth Portfolio and Performance forms synchronization
entry.content:
Hi @Luka 

thanks for your explaination.

I have two questions:

- Competencies or Skills (both in Talent Hub) tagged as "Core" (Custom Tag with code Core) are populated into the performance form. This does NOT work for "Job Specific". So if we want to populate the performance forms with "Job Specific" Competencies, a custom Tag with "Job Specific" does NOT work. Do you have a tip here?

- Upon completion of performance process the Competencies and Skills are refleced in the subject's Growth Portfolio together with the ratings as expected, BUT the subject can still edit the rating in the Growth Portfolio. That seems not to work as expected. Can this be changed?

Thanks and BR

Martin

entry.title: Does Two-step picking feature require EWM advanced license?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

According to the below blog, WAVE management is included in during Two-step picking customization process. 

In my understanding, WAVE management feature requires EWM advanced license.

Does Two-step picking feature also require EWM advanced license?

Best regards,

Arata

 

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-members/two-step-picking-in-sap-ewm/ba-p/13512803

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Recruiting: New Career Site Job Search and Job Layout
entry.content:
Thanks a lot @LisaRaff for the update regarding radial search.

@HangTRAN- I am afraid we did the same MANUAL update of the Jobs for our test system and will do so for our upcoming customer project. I know that is not ideal but for now the most pragmatic approach. Let us keep each other updated

entry.title: Re: A Waring message is displayed when creating an error...
entry.content:
@sebastian_wilhelm1

According to the blog post below,

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/exposing-bapi-as-odata-api-using-rap-facade/ba-p/13571926

To handle the BAPI errors, you must change the inheritance of your local saver class definition from "cl_abap_behavior_saver" to "cl_abap_behavior_saver_failed".
This will enable the additional parameter "failed" in method "adjust_numbers".See RAP Saver Classes and Methods in ABAP Keyword Documentation for more details

Does your save_modified method have the parameter 'failed'? If not, try inheriting from cl_abap_behavior_saver_failed and setting 'failed-header'.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
@MK0024 Welcome Mathew! How do you like being an SAP Consultant? In case you don't know already, we have a couple of location groups for Austria: Wien and Zürich - please feel free to join either or both of those groups and connect locally. There's also a group for St. Gallen (Switzerland) depending on which part of Austria you live in - it might be near.

Be sure to take part in discussions in our groups! We'd love to hear from you.

entry.title: Cloud ALM outbound raw data API - filter by job name using wildcard or partial name
entry.content:
Hi, I'm exploring the usage of the metrics API and I want to fetch a list of jobs filtering by a partial job name, something like *PartialJobName*.

In the Jobs and Automation monitoring app filter, this is the default behavior and I inspected the GET request which shows the how the filter uses "contains" as in the below example:

GET JobMonitorView?$count=true&$orderby=ExecutionStatus%20desc&$filter=ServiceId%20eq%20'XXXX'%20and%20contains(JobName,'PartialJobName')%20and%20RuntimeUpperThreshold%20eq%204%20and%20RuntimeLowerThreshold%20eq%202%20and%20DelayUpperThreshold%20eq%2025%20and%20DelayLowerThreshold%20eq%2010%20and%20PlannedStartTime%20ge%202024-03-05T11:00:53.452Z%20and%20PlannedStartTime%20le%202024-03-07T11:00:53.452Z&$skip=0&$top=127 HTTP/1.1

I'm trying to achieve the same result for the raw data API but so far, I didn't succeed and I'm not sure if it's possible when reading the data provider documentation, as indicated in the raw data API filters options. 

This is an example of a request that works 

{{CALM_URL}}/calm-metrics/v1/metrics?version=V1&period=L24H&provider=jam&dimensions=serviceName,jobName&metrics=measure:aborted&serviceId=XXXXXXXX&jobName='OnlyWorksWithCompleteJobName'

I tried to add remove the jobName parameter and add the filter parameter as in the J&A app, but it didn't work. Something like this: &filter=contains(jobName,'PartialJobName').

I tried other combinations using the jobName parameter and "*" or "%" too, but it didn't work. Maybe someone can help to confirm if this option is even available since the documentation is not clear to me.

entry.title: Empowering the Energy and Utilities Industry in Seville, Spain
entry.content:
The energy and utilities industries are undergoing a transformative shift, driven by technological advancements and the need for sustainable solutions. To stay ahead of the curve and gain valuable insights into the future of this sector, the SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference is the event to attend. This premier gathering of industry leaders, experts, and innovators promises to be a game-changer, offering a platform for knowledge-sharing, networking, and exploring the latest trends in the field.  Join us as we delve into the exciting world of energy and utilities, powered by SAP's cutting-edge solutions.

Presented by TAC Insights, this year the event will take place in beautiful Seville, Spain, from April 23-25, under a theme of, “Energy for a brighter future”.  The first day is considered “pre-conference”, where you can hone your skills and enjoy deep-dive workshops and open-door clinics with expert guidance on implementation tips and best practices.  The main conference will be held the following two days where you can explore 40+ real-life SAP user case studies, visit with 40+ exhibitors, attend microforums, and network with over 900 industry players.

There is also a welcome reception, businesswomen’s networking lunch, and of course the legendary “celebration evening”, which is always a memorable time at the event.

Reasons to attend

1. Unparalleled Networking Opportunities:
The SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference brings together professionals from various sectors of the industry, including energy providers, utilities companies, technology experts, and thought leaders. This event provides an exceptional opportunity to connect with like-minded individuals, exchange ideas, and forge valuable partnerships that can drive innovation and growth.

2. Insights from Industry Leaders:
Gain valuable insights from industry leaders who have successfully navigated the challenges and opportunities in the energy and utilities sector. Keynote speakers and panel discussions will shed light on the latest trends, best practices, and strategies for sustainable growth. Learn from their experiences and apply their knowledge to your own organization.

3. Discover SAP's Cutting-Edge Solutions:
As a leader in enterprise software, SAP offers innovative solutions tailored specifically for the energy and utilities industry. At the conference, you will have the chance to explore SAP's latest offerings, including advanced analytics, machine learning, and Internet of Things (IoT) technologies. Discover how these solutions can optimize your operations, improve efficiency, and enhance customer experiences.

4. Engaging Sessions and Workshops:
The conference agenda is packed with engaging sessions, workshops, and interactive discussions designed to address the most pressing challenges faced by the industry. From digital transformation to sustainability initiatives, regulatory compliance to customer engagement, there is something for everyone. Learn from industry experts, participate in hands-on workshops, and gain practical knowledge that you can implement in your organization.

5. Stay Ahead of the Curve:
In an industry that is constantly evolving, staying ahead of the curve is essential for success. The SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference offers a unique opportunity to stay updated on the latest trends, emerging technologies, and regulatory changes.

6.  Gain insights from customer stories in our packed agenda:

  • Hear about customer co-innovation from the CFO of Cepsa and CEO of PowerLink
  • Learn how Farys is innovating in smart metering alert handling through cutting-edge technology
  • See how Motor Oil manages a substantial investment plan for expanding in new activities and acquiring companies in new businesses
  • Hear how Edison successfully migrated to SAP S/4HANA, Ariba, and BTP to great results
  • Learn about Oxy’s bold steps to innovate to a lower-carbon future utilizing RISE with SAP
  • Hear about Aquafin’s digital transformation of field operations with SAP Field Service Management
  • See how Chevron enabled a systemic maintenance strategy to optimize asset performance
  • Learn from Contact Energy about their modernization of core systems for digital transformation and sustainable growth
  • Hear about ConocoPhillips’ transformation of field logistics 
  • See how ENEL unified their approach to asset management
  • And many more!

Register here for the SAP Energy and Utilities Conference now!

We hope to see you there.

entry.title: Re: EWM - Picking WT creation fails due lack of stock
entry.content: Thanks for writing. I dont have any batch selection criteria.

entry.title: Re: Different result when call BAPI Create MO (IW31)
entry.content:
(I didn't mention program difference, but missing Customizing)

So, did you check for missing/inconsistent Customizing, for example

Transaction SPRO > IMG > Plant Maintenance and Customer Service > Maintenance and Service Processing > Configuration Control > Define Attributes for System Messages

entry.title: IAS is mandatory for customers with Microsoft Azure SSO login
entry.content: I have a query for customer with Azure SSO login. If we can activate IAS for all customers including Azure SSO login

entry.title: Re: EWM - Picking WT creation fails due lack of stock
entry.content: Exact Error is - Restricted batch stock have skipped for process type Z201

could you tell me if you have fixed this issue?

entry.title: Define Custom Routines for Data Transfer to Billing Documents (BADI SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER usage)
entry.content:
Hi,

In S4HANA Public Cloud System while creating new Billing Document in VF01 i want to trigger  SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER (Custom Data Transfer for Billing Documents) BADI.

This BADI have a filter DATA_TRANSFER. To use the BADI i need to set up some configuration for this filter. Here is a Documentation description: 

Does anybody know how to do this configuration? I cannot find any application like this

  • Define Custom Routines for Data Transfer to Billing Documents

Have anybody already used this BADI?

Regards

Vitali

entry.title: RFC_CALL_TRANSACTION_USING
entry.content:
Hello together

Use case:
I call the module RFC_CALL_TRANSACTION_USING from my external application via RFC.
I would like to call the transaction "IE03" and display it in the GUI.
I also transfer the equipment no.

Result:
The GUI window opens in the transaction with the correct equipment

Problem:
The GUI window can no longer be closed until I close my external application.

Code:

public void CALLTRANSACTION(string TCode, string EQNr)
{

// Rufe die SAP Einstellungen auf
var sapDestination = sapConnector.GetDestination2();

// Funktionsbaustein aufrufen
var function_Transaction_Call = sapDestination.Repository.CreateFunction("RFC_CALL_TRANSACTION_USING");

// Setze die Parameter des Funktionsbausteins
function_Transaction_Call.SetValue("TCODE", TCode);
function_Transaction_Call.SetValue("MODE", "E");

// Struktur vorbereiten
var BT_DATA = function_Transaction_Call.GetTable("BT_DATA");

BT_DATA.Clear();
BT_DATA.Append();

BT_DATA.SetValue("PROGRAM", "SAPMIEQ0");
BT_DATA.SetValue("DYNPRO", "0100");
BT_DATA.SetValue("DYNBEGIN", "X");

BT_DATA.Append();

BT_DATA.SetValue("FNAM", "BDC_CURSOR");
BT_DATA.SetValue("FVAL", "RM63E-EQUNR");

BT_DATA.Append();

BT_DATA.SetValue("FNAM", "BDC_OKCODE");
BT_DATA.SetValue("FVAL", "/00");

BT_DATA.Append();

BT_DATA.SetValue("FNAM", "RM63E-EQUNR");
BT_DATA.SetValue("FVAL", EQNr);

function_Transaction_Call.Invoke(sapDestination);

}

entry.title: Re: Send HTML E-Mails using SAP Build
entry.content: H Chaitanya_Priya, some parts of the scripts are cut out, is there a blog on the complete steps to enable easy replication of what you posted? If it is available, can you share the link to the blog?

entry.title: Re: delete serial numbers from inbound delivery
entry.content: May I know if you have got the solution for removing serial number from delivery

entry.title: SAP Companion - Extended content not displaying in Chrome
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I've got a rather curious question. When our S/4HANA environment is accessed via Chrome, standard SAP Companion content is displayed, but extended content isn't.

The problem doesn't manifest when accessing S/4HANA via Edge. 

Does anybody know what the reason for this may be?

Thanks so much,

Andrew

entry.title: Re: Identity Provisioning: How to Get Users Based on Group Assignments from MS Azure AD
entry.content:
thanks a lot! I did solve this problem after I had posted my comment. but I've used a different approach

I am using aad.group.filter and later in target system with a condition in transformation I make sure that only group members replicated

{
"user": {
"condition": "($.emails EMPTY false) && ($.userName EMPTY false) && isValidEmail($.emails[0].value) && ($.groups[?(@.displayName == '...')] EMPTY false)",

good to know that there is an another way thanks a lot!

entry.title: Table with Authorization Roles and Organizational Levels
entry.content:
Hello experts , 

i want to take from SE16N list with Roles and values for organizational Levels.

The result that i want to see in list i need to have columns : Name Role , Values from Organizational levels.

If i cant take this result in which table can i have roles and authorizations objects with Fields ?

Thanks a lot.

entry.title: Re: SAP Companion - Extended content not displaying in Ch...
entry.content:
D'oh! It was because I hadn't logged in to Enable Now Manager via Chrome. 

So simple!

entry.title: Busca de pedido de venda pela data de cancelamento / Sales order search by cancellation date.
entry.content:
Bom dia Pessoal,

Estou montando uma query para uma relatório para exibir os pedidos cancelados, e a premissa seria a busca pela data de cancelamento do pedido.

No caso no SAP B1, nas tabelas envolvendo a ORDR e suas filhas, não possuem a informação da data do cancelamento do pedido, porem encontro ela na tabela ADOC (de logs), quando o updatedate é referente a alteração da coluna CANCELED para Y.

Alguém já conseguiu fazer um relatório/query que busque pela data de cancelamento ou conseguiu contornar de alguma forma, para que fosse possível fazer a busca por essa data?

Atenciosamente,
Vinicius Dezan.

 

In English:

Good morning,

I'm putting together a query for a report to display canceled orders, and the premise would be to search for the order cancellation date.

In the case of SAP B1, the tables involving the ORDR and its children do not have information on the order cancellation date, but I can find it in the ADOC table (logs), when the updatedate refers to the change of the CANCELED column to Y.

Has anyone managed to create a report/query that searches for the cancellation date or managed to get around it in some way, so that it would be possible to search for that date?

Yours sincerely,
Vinicius Dezan.

entry.title: How long will SuccessFactors still support / offer Plateau Report Designer?
entry.content:
Dear All,

has anyone heard about the future of the Plateau Report Designer? Will this end, once People Analytics is more established? Like the "old" Report Center reports?

Thanks, Uwe

entry.title: Image is not showing in Interaction Center in CRM/SSF and Formatting is losing in SOIN transaction
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

When I send an email to SAP, I can see image and formatting in outlook:

When I open SOIN transaction I can see the image, but formatting was lost:

 

and when I open SSF and check Interaction Center I can see the formatting, but I cannot see the image:

 

Could you help me, please?

I tried these and doesn't work:

- SAP NOTES:

3236904 IC : Image in standard response is lost after preview
3248901 IC: Javascript issues in the email component
3238610 Interaction Center: Filename description with special characters cause session error
3329061 Images not displayed when replying or forwarding emails in the email editor
3021392 Buttons "Apply", "Preview" and "Add to favorites" greyed out in Standard Response
3364304 Runtime error CALL_FUNCTION_REMOTE_ERROR during attempt to send an e-mail
3351913 Style of inbound e-mail not considered in reply
3353101 CRM-IC: Default language for favorites standards responses base on customer preferences
3353757 Inbound e-mail not immediately displayed as completed after reply
3321690 Upgrade of jQuery version 3.6.3 in WEBCUIF Framework - Adding debugging capability
3311839 Upgrade of jQuery version 3.6.3 in WEBCUIF Framework
3377069 Add custom parameter support in CL_THTMLB_UTIL
3388974 TinyMCE: Enhanced support for multiple TinyMCE versions
3374505 TinyMCE: Newly added text shows bigger line spacing
3355642 TinyMCE: Editor text lost when saving the web page
3386313 TinyMCE: Customization support enhancement based on note 3374505
3322293 CRM-IC: jQuery version 3.6.3

- Define E-Mail Profiles

Path in SPRO transaction: Service -> Interaction Center WebClient -> Basic Functions -> Communication Channels -> Define E-Mail Profiles

And I can see it in console google chrome:

Thanks a lot for your attentions 🙂

entry.title: Re: BADI for billing creation
entry.content:
Hi,

If this is still relevant, probably i have a Developer Extensibility (3SL system) workaround solution for you.

Not sure about this particular BADI, but solution is definitely working for the similar  case: FIN_ACDOC_ITEM_SUBSTITUTION BADI, that get triggered for every line item, and within a BADI you don't see other line items values. 

So, this workaround is based on the assumption that all BADIs for ALL LINE ITEMS GET TRIGGERED IN SINGLE RUNTIME SESSION. If so, solution is pretty simple.

You need to create a Singleton class with a lifetime limited by one Billing Document creation. 

Imagine, you have 3 line items for Billing Document, so your SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER BADI works 3 times (within one runtime session).

For first line (first BADI triggering) you create your Singleton instance and store there current line item values.

For next lines (next BADI triggering) you get existing Singleton instance, add value of current line item and also you can check values from other line items. 

Implementation steps are the following:

1 In ADT create Singleton class, it can looks like:

CLASS zcl_singleton DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    CLASS-METHODS get_instance
      RETURNING
        VALUE(ro_instance) TYPE REF TO zcl_singleton .

    METHODS add_lineitem
      IMPORTING
        is_lineitem TYPE <YOUR_LINE_ITEM>.

    METHODS get_lineitems
      RETURNING VALUE(rt_lineitems) TYPE <YOUR_LINE_ITEM_TAB>.

  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    CLASS-DATA mo_instance TYPE REF TO zcl_singleton .
    DATA mt_lineitem TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF <YOUR_LINE_ITEM_TAB>.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_acdoc_fins_item_subs IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD get_instance.
    IF mo_instance IS NOT BOUND.
      mo_instance = NEW zcl_singleton( ).
    ENDIF.
    ro_instance = mo_instance.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD add_lineitem.
    " TODO add new line item
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_lineitems
    rt_lineitems = mt_lineitem.
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

2 Release this class for Key User Extensibility. You can call this class from you BADI.

  1. Go to class Properties -> API State -> Use System-Internally (Contract 1) -> Add.
  2. Choose:
    • Release State: Released
    • Use in Key User Apps
  3. Add it to Transport Request.

3 Implement your BADI logic. It can looks like: 

* Your BADI Logic ...

* Create Instance of you buffer class - for first line item of Billing Document
* Or get existing one - for other line items
DATA(lo_buffer) = zcl_singleton=>get_instance( ). 

* Add current line item values 
lo_buffer->add_lineitem( <YOUR_LINE_ITEM> ).

* Get all line items for entire document (that has been added to the buffer already)
DATA(lt_line_items) = lo_buffer->get_lineimtes( ).

* Do whatever logic you need with your Billing Document

Hope it helpful.

Best Regards

Vitali

entry.title: Re: How To Upload Data to a Public or Private Planning Version Using a File Upload Custom Widget Wi
entry.content:
Hi @Privete_member__916507 ,

Actually we are currently working on an 'in product' solution for the file upload. I hope we can give you some more details soon when this solution will be finally shipped.

Best regards

Gerd

entry.title: Starting Career in SAP HCM ECC 6.0 in 2023
entry.content:
Hello Experts !!

I have started career in SAP HCM ECC 6.0 in starting month of 2023, and worked till 1 year on support projects now i am not working because of less job opportunity with 1 yr experience, mostly opportunities are for 3-5 years & above with implementation exp. 

Now I am worried about future in SAP HCM on premises system, due to very less job opportunities in entry level jobs, and SAP also goes to stop provide support services after 2027 for ECC.

1) should I continue with SAP HCM or move to another could based technologies like SuccessFactors , workday etc.?

2) what if I got job on support SAP HCM Consultant role for gov. client should I work? 

your opinion will really help me to get out from this complexity.

Thanks,

Vikas

entry.title: Re: CRM Mashup Configuration in C4C
entry.content: Very Informative blog on the Mash-ups.

entry.title: Re: Need to delete serial number from outbound delivery created in MIGO

(Use of where-used search is mandatory when using  unreleased FM)

You could try a code similar to

    PERFORM sn_status_operation_determine(sapfv50w)
                                  USING    likp-vbtyp
                                           tvlk-aufer
                                           lips
                                           charx
                                  CHANGING lf_vorgang.

entry.title: Re: VA02 from an ABAP program
entry.content: @SantoshB you can update conditions using the BAPI if that is what you are asking (although what you are asking is not clear), but that also will depend on how the pricing procedure is configured.

entry.title: Re: Bidirectional integration between SAP SuccessFactors & Azure Active Directory in Microsoft Azure
entry.content:
Thanks @Sebastian_W ! Seems that this will be the only solution. 

FYI we also tried to map country code (which is an attribute that should be supported as stated in the documentation), but that is not working either. When using the picklist value of a certain country code, the app can make the translation to the correct code, but in the end we get an error message:

entry.title: Tax posting using BAPI - BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST
entry.content:
Introduction:

 BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST allows you to post accounting entries like GL postings and customer or Vendor postings (FB50, FB60, FB70, FB01) but we cannot do the clearing using this BAPI. Through this blog, I like to explain to you all about the tax posting and how we must handle 0% and other payable % taxes while posting.

Scenarios:

  1. Customer posting with 0% and 5% tax posting.
  2. GL posting with 0% and 5 % tax posting.

Procedures:

  1. Go to SE37 execute – BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST.
  2. Document header – To fill in all header details.
  3. Account GL – To fill GL accounts with taxes depending on the scenarios.
  4. Accounts receivable / Accounts payable – To fill in customer/vendor posting.
  5. Account Tax – To fill for all tax line items.
  6. Currency Amount – Enter amount for all line items.
  7. Commit Posting – BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT.

Scenario 1 – Customer Posting with 0% and 5% tax posting:

 It is mandatory to fill in all header details and account receivable details related to a customer.

For 0% tax, as it is nil tax there is no need to fill in details in the Account Tax table, but we need to provide the tax rate only in the Account GL table which should have an impact In F.12 report.

For other % taxes, it may be 5 or 10% it is mandatory to provide tax code details in the Account GL table and tax details in the Account Tax table. Let’s see the test scenario via the below screenshots.

0% Tax rate Scenario – Customer Invoice

These are some mandatory header details to be filled.

 

 

Item 1: Accounts receivables

 

Item 2: Account GL – As mentioned above for 0% or nil tax it is enough to maintain the tax code here.

 

 

Currency amount to be filled for items 1 – Accounts receivable to be debited & item 2 – Account GL – Revenue account to be credited. The negative sign to the amount will identify the credit value. The amount with positive will be the debit value.

For posting documents, we need to Commit the function module using BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT

Se37 – Menu – Function module – Test – test Sequence.

 

 

After Executing – Press back.

 

Impact on F.12 Report:

5% Tax rate Scenario – Customer Invoice

Item 1 – Accounts Receivable

Item 2: GL accounts with tax rates

 

Item 3 – Account Tax – As I mentioned earlier other than 0% and nil Tax, the tax account details to be filled account tax table.

Currency Amount – for the tax rate payable – in currency amount table 3-line items will be maintained. First-line item will be the customer item with the debit value along with the tax amount. Second-line item will be a GL item with a credit value followed by a negative amount. Third-line item will be the tax item with a negative tax amount and in the amount base field, the tax base amount must be maintained positive.

As earlier, we need to commit and post the document.

Impact In F.12 Report:

Scenario 2: GL posting with 0% and 5 % tax posting.

Here in GL posting, we going to post both 0 and 5% Tax rates in the same document. Let’s see its impact.

Now I am going to post the revenue receipt GL posting. The entry will incoming bank GL will be debited, and revenue accounts will be credited.

Bank a/c Dr

Revenue1 a/c Cr – 5% tax rate

Revenue2 a/c Dr – 0% tax rate

Header details

Account GL – As it is revenue receipt posting, all 3-line items will be fall under this category.

In account, GL tax code (0&5%) must be entered in both revenue GL. In the Account tax table, it is enough to provide details only for tax 5%.

Item 1: Revenue1 GL – 5%

Item 2: Revenue2 GL – 0%

Item 3: Bank GL

 

Account Tax: The details will be maintained only for a tax rate of 5%.

Currency amount: The First line item contains a 5% tax rate for which line item 4 contains tax value and its base amount. The second line item contains a 0% tax rate revenue item, and the third line item will be the cumulative value of bank GL with debit value.

As in like the previous posting we need to commit and post the values.

 

Impact of Taxes on GL Posting:

 

Conclusion:

I hope I have explained in detail the BAPI account posting with taxes. This blog will be helpful for all who use BAPI posting along with taxes.

entry.title: S4-FSCM-Activate Credit Management Basic Steps
entry.content:
Customers/Business Partner plays a vital role in Company’s performance.  Hence it is very important to maintain and monitor the Business Partners data.  Maintenance of Credit for the Business Partner is very critical which helps to reduces the risk of Financial losses.  SAP's Credit Management provides the easier and efficient way by maintaining the centralized credit management.   

Now we can see the steps required to Active the Credit Management with the SPRO Navigation path and with the required BADI methods that needs to be implemented.

Step 1 :- Activate the SAP Credit Management using the BADI - UKM_R3_ACTIVATE

IMG Path : SPRO-> Financial Supply Chain Management -> Credit Management -> Integration with Accounts Receivable Accounting and Sales and Distribution -> Integration with Accounts Receivable accounting -> BAdi: Activation of SAP Credit Management

Execute the option BAdi: Activation of SAP Credit Management to create the Enhancement Implementation 

Choose Continue

Choose Creation of Enhancement

Create Implementation for the below Interface Methods .  You can refer the example class  CL_EXM_IM_UKM_R3_ACTIVATE and below sample code.

==================================================================== Sample Code                          ====================================================================

Method : IF_EX_UKM_R3_ACTIVATE~SET_ACTIVE

 

 

 

METHOD if_ex_ukm_r3_activate~set_active.

    e_active_flag = 'X'.
    e_erp2005 = 'X'.
ENDMETHOD.

 

 

 

Method : IF_EX_UKM_R3_ACTIVATE~ FI_AR_UPDATE_MODE

Method : IF_EX_UKM_R3_ACTIVATE~DCD_ACTIVE

 

 

 

Method : IF_EX_UKM_R3_ACTIVATE~DCD_ACTIVE

  METHOD if_ex_ukm_r3_activate~dcd_active.

    e_dcd_active = 'X'.

  ENDMETHOD.

 

 

 

Method : IF_EX_UKM_R3_ACTIVATE~GET_RFCDEST_FSCM

Method : IF_EX_UKM_R3_ACTIVATE~NO_SLD

Activate the Enhancement Implementation ZENH_ACTIVATE_CM

Choose Back And activate the BADI Implementation

Activate Badi: Connection of SD to SAP Credit Management

IMG Path : SPRO-> Financial Supply Chain Management -> Credit Management -> Integration with Accounts Receivable Accounting and Sales and Distribution -> Integration with Sales and Distribution -> Customer Enhancements -> BAdi: Connection of SD to SAP Credit Management

 

Method : IF_EX_BADI_SD_CM~FSCM_COMMITMENT_UPDATE_ORDER, can be customized to update the Credit exposure for the Sales Order based on business requirement.

Now we have activated the Credit Management in FSCM, In the next blog., we can see how to perform credit check and the basic requirement/Configuration parameters required for credit check...

Tried to provide detailed explanation....

Thanks You all SAP Experts...

entry.title: POD & Valuated Stock in Transit Configuration
entry.content:
The agenda of this blog is to enable the process of making the COGS (Cost of goods sold) hit at the later stage of delivery i.e once the stock is handed over to the customer.

 

In order to execute this we are required to make use of concept called Valuated stock in transit where ‘Stock in transit’ refers to the goods that have left the physical plant, but the control of them isn’t transferred to the buyer at the same time. These goods are still in the control of the company that ships the goods and are still part of the valuated stock of that company.

 

This process where the stock is been transferred to valuated stock in transit is then been used to hit COGS at the time of POD ( Proof of delivery ) by which invoicing can be carried out immediately right after the confirmation from customer on stock received where by invoicing and cogs can be made to hit right after each other.

Change the movement type field in schedule line with ‘687’ from ‘601’ GM del to issue valuated stock in transit. This field helps in transferring the goods to valuated stock in transit instead of hitting the cogs.

Maintain Movement type issue valuated SiT with the movement type which hits COGS (601) so this accounting would be carried out at the time of POD.

While executing the transaction make sure you have activated the customer as POD relevant and maintained the necessary configuration for POD on item categories as well. When enabled POST GOODS ISSUE button at top would have been disabled at the time of creation of delivery where by we are required to go to change mode to post goods issue which creates an accounting document as mentioned below.

And after saving the POD document you can find the COGS been hit in following document which would have been created.

And we can carry out invoicing as we do and document flow is given for reference.

entry.title: CAD indicator (MARA-CADKZ) in material master after material creation
entry.content:
Hello
I have created a simplistic 3D model from the CAD desktop and asked to associate a MM to it:
A document and the associated MM were created.
The tabs of the material have been filled in automatically, but in the basic data 2 tab , the CAD indicator is not checked (this indicates that an item has been created by CAD interface). Could you please tell me which custo is missing in the cdesk_cus transaction? I haven't found anything.
In addition, I see in the "Purchasing" tab that the Plant-sp.matl status field is at status 03 when it should be at "awaiting validation (98)". Could you please tell me which custo is missing in the cdesk_cus transaction?
Thanks in advance

BR

entry.title: Re: FRUN/RUM - filtering user locations
entry.content:
Hello BGABruins,
There is currently no option to display requests of users that are NOT in table USRLOC as only a INNER Join is used. 

What Romy displayed above in the (LMDB) scope selector is an LMDB (customer) attribute called Location that has been created in LMDB. This has nothing to do with the locations of RUM in table USRLOC.

Best regards, 
Thomas

entry.title: Re: Data Purge and Anonymization: SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting
entry.content:
Hi Kim,

Thanks for the info. I have an additional question. We have set the number of purging days for both application and candidate profile to 28 days. The profile will not be purged if there is an active application.

If the candidate is moved to an inactive status after 25days, the application will be purged 28 days later. At the same time the candidate profile is flagged to be anomimyzed and will be purged after 3 days. The last login date is the start of counting the days for candidate profile purge.

Is it possible to start counting from the moment the candidate is moved to an inactive status for the profile purge? Thank you! Renee

entry.title: The Transformation Imperative - The role of technology in keeping your business relevant
entry.content:
In this session, we will host Vijay Sethi, Chairman INDUS and he will delve into the critical role that SAP technology can play in ensuring the relevance of your business amidst evolving landscapes. This session illuminates how technology serves as the cornerstone for staying relevant, driving innovation, and competitive edge in modern markets. He will share strategic insights that will help participants succeed with their SAP S/4HANA led transformation projects.

Speaker/s:
Mukesh Gupta, Vice President Customer Advocacy, SAP
Vijay Sethi, Chairman Indian Users of SAP (INDUS)

Registration link: https://events.sap.com/transformation/en/home/#240416

entry.title: Re: delete serial numbers from inbound delivery
entry.content:
(Use of where-used search is mandatory when using  unreleased FM)

You could try a code similar to

 

    PERFORM sn_status_operation_determine(sapfv50w)
                                  USING    likp-vbtyp
                                           tvlk-aufer
                                           lips
                                           charx
                                  CHANGING lf_vorgang.

entry.title: Differentiating Your Core ERP with SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA differs significantly from SAP ECC / Business Suite in several aspects. This session provides an overview of key differentiators and benefits of SAP S/4HANA with regard to new and significantly improved business processes, technical architecture, the new user experience (Fiori), analytics, and last but not least artificial intelligence.

Speaker/s: Michael Deller, Chief Solution Expert - SAP S/4HANA Solution Management, SAP

Registration link: https://events.sap.com/transformation/en/home/#240423

entry.title: Re: Image is not showing in Interaction Center in CRM/SSF and Formatting is losing in SOIN transacti
entry.content: And I can see it in console in google chrome:

entry.title: Re: Image is not showing in Interaction Center in CRM/SSF...
entry.content: And I can see it in console google chrome:

entry.title: Exploring the Basics of SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
This session starts with a brief overview of the most important differences between SAP ERP 6.0 and SAP S/4HANA around the most important concepts, such as: Simplifications, Compatibility Pack Scope, and SAP S/4HANA Release & Product Strategy, as well as the different migration approaches. Learn the key criteria for choosing the most appropriate migration approach for you. We discuss the most important activities that you need to complete for a successful migration project. We will also highlight key tools and skills that will support you in the planning, preparation, and execution of your  migration project.

Speaker/s: Roland Hamm, Product Manager SAP S/4HANA, SAP

Registration link: https://events.sap.com/transformation/en/home/#240430

entry.title: Challenge Your Business Processes
entry.content:
To ensure that the transition to SAP S/4HANA creates added value for your company, you should challenge existing business processes and improve them – either as part of your migration project or in subsequent optimization projects. It is also important for the choice of your individual transformation path and for realistic project planning to decide to what extent processes are to be reused or redesigned. In this online session, you will learn about the important questions and procedures.

Speaker/s: Michael Sokollek, Senior Director, Strategic Customer Transformation, SAP Signavio

Registration link: https://events.sap.com/transformation/en/home/#240507

entry.title: Re: shipping metrics: lines shipped & pieces shipped.
entry.content:
Some tables related to shipments / deliveries

LIKPSD Document: Delivery Header Data
LIPSSD document: Delivery: Item data
VBFASales Document Flow
VFKKshipment Costs: Header Data
VFKPshipment Costs: Item Data
VTFAshipment Document Flow
VTSPStage of Transport / Item Allocation
VTTKshipment Header
VTTPshipment Item
VTTSStage of shipment

But you should first execute some standard transactions with a SQL trace (ST05)

entry.title: Lessons learned from successful S/4HANA transformation projects
entry.content:
The Regional Implementation Group has supported over 1,300 projects on its journey to SAP S/4HANA. Based on the experience from these projects, this webinar will present the most important factors and lessons learned for a successful SAP S/4HANA transformation.

  • Key aspects to consider for a successful SAP S/4HANA transformation
  • What are the recommended next steps to get started
  • What offers from RIG you can support in your project

Speaker/s: 
Tina Gailus and Christine Sievi, both SAP S/4HANA Customer Care & Regional Implementation Group (RIG), SAP

Registration link: https://events.sap.com/transformation/en/home/#240514

entry.title: Re: Blog it Forward – Pierre Col
entry.content:
Thank you so much Erick, I'm touched by your message and your proposal. 🙏🏻

Unfortunately, despite having been 3 times in Colombia and many times in Spain, my Spanish is too weak for this kind of conversation: I can read a written article in a newspaper and have a bit of interaction in a grocery or at a restaurant or a museum, but not at the level required for your interviews...

Let's say that this is another motivation for further improving my Spanish, because I like your mother tongue, especially with the smooth South American accent...

entry.title: PDI - SAP Cloud Application Studio 2402 (Pilot) - "One or more breakpoints could not be set"
entry.content:
Hello,

I have often the problem in SAP Cloud Application Studio that breakpoints are not set. Even if I save and activate the file again and I start the Studio again and log-off and log-in again with the user. I also refreshed the Solution mentioned here https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3043406 

It is a original solution in corection and test environment:

Why is it so complicated to set a breakpoint compared to an ABAP Editor? And how to fix this issue?

Thanks and best regards,

Julian
